meaning: adj.外形的,正式的,合礼仪的,形式的,整齐匀称的n.正式的社交活动
examples: The dinner was a formal affair.
She completed her formal education in 1995.
The consultation paper should pave the way for formal guidance to all final-salary pension schemes.
Bike theft also is common, as there is no formal registration for private bikes.
